humanSummary.text: The repaired target span mapping work now aligns Rust encoded-slot, relocation, fixture compatibility, and TypeScript bridge validation for duplicate raw protected spans: duplicates require valid non-reused source identity, while reordered distinct mappings remain accepted. No P0/P1 findings remain. One non-blocking legacy PatchExport compatibility risk remains.

findings.title: Legacy PatchExport duplicate-span compatibility check
findings.description: The fixture adapter now enforces duplicate raw protected-span source identity in the legacy Rust PatchExport patch path as well as the v0.2 compatibility path. Generated paths include identity and v0.1 bridge serialization is unchanged, but externally-authored legacy patch exports that previously relied only on distinct target ranges may now fail unless they include the newly optional identity fields.

evidence.detail: Re-audit confirmed the original P1 is fixed across Rust core, fixture compatibility, relocation, and TypeScript, then isolated this as a compatibility-scope risk for externally-authored legacy PatchExport inputs.

cargo test -p kaifuu-core duplicate_raw_protected_spans; cargo test -p kaifuu-engine-fixture duplicate_raw_protected_spans_require; pnpm exec vitest run packages/localization-bridge-schema/test/schema.test.ts

findings.impact: Legacy patch authors could see a stricter failure mode without a clear migration path if they duplicate raw protected spans and do not provide source identity.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Document or test the intended compatibility behavior for legacy PatchExport duplicate raw protected spans.
If legacy distinct-target-range behavior must remain supported, add a compatibility path that preserves safety while avoiding false rejection.
If strict identity is intended for legacy inputs, provide an explicit diagnostic and migration guidance for adding source identity fields.

proposedDagNode.title: Legacy PatchExport duplicate-span compatibility
proposedDagNode.summary: Clarify and harden legacy PatchExport behavior for duplicate raw protected spans after v0.2 source identity validation.
deliverables: Documented legacy PatchExport duplicate-span compatibility policy
Regression coverage for externally-authored legacy duplicate raw protected-span mappings
acceptanceCriteria: Legacy PatchExport duplicate raw protected-span behavior is explicitly documented as either strict identity-required or compatibility-preserving.
Externally-authored legacy fixtures cover duplicate raw spans with and without source identity and assert the intended diagnostics.
The v0.2 bridge and Rust compatibility paths continue rejecting missing, wrong, or reused identity for duplicate raw spans.
auditFocus: No regression in v0.2 source identity validation
Diagnostics guide patch authors toward safe source identity
